_description: Patterns commonly considered sensitive PII under EU jurisdictions (notably GDPR).
_disclaimer: Using this profile does not constitute compliance with GDPR or any framework. It only enables redaction of patterns commonly considered sensitive in the named jurisdiction.

patterns.name: eu-iban
patterns.notes: ISO 13616 shape: 2-letter country, 2 check digits, 11-30 alphanumeric BBAN. No checksum validator in v0.5.0 (Luhn-style mod-97 deferred); narrow length keeps false-positive rate manageable.

patterns.name: nl-bsn
patterns.notes: Dutch BSN is 9 digits with an 11-test checksum, but 9-digit substrings false-positive constantly. Requiring an explicit 'BSN' prefix is the cheapest narrowing.

patterns.name: it-codice-fiscale
patterns.notes: Italian fiscal code: 6 letters, 2 digits, 1 letter, 2 digits, 1 letter, 3 digits, 1 letter. Distinctive shape; low false-positive rate.

patterns.name: fr-insee
patterns.notes: French INSEE/NIR 13-digit social-security number, with optional 2-digit cle (key) for the 15-digit form. Requires explicit 'INSEE' or 'NIR' prefix to avoid catching arbitrary 13- or 15-digit numbers.
